old_claim.note: Foraging & Botany provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The Xianrendong makers were foragers, but botanical foraging knowledge is their subsistence context rather than a technical requirement for preparing clay.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract: dates Xianrendong pottery to 20,000-19,000 calendar years BP and reports that mobile hunter-gatherers produced it long before agriculture.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Mobile hunter-gatherers at Xianrendong produced pottery 20,000-19,000 calendar years BP, before agriculture.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped evidence establishes the milestone without the removed dependency. The Xianrendong makers were foragers, but botanical foraging knowledge is their subsistence context rather than a technical requirement for preparing clay.

ontology_before: clay_gathering_and_preparation treated foraging_and_botany as a direct causal prerequisite.
ontology_after: clay_gathering_and_preparation is scoped to the directly evidenced mechanism and no longer claims foraging_and_botany as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: clay_gathering_and_preparation no longer directly depends on foraging_and_botany.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that every implementation within the current clay_gathering_and_preparation scope technically requires foraging_and_botany.
short_reason: The Xianrendong makers were foragers, but botanical foraging knowledge is their subsistence context rather than a technical requirement for preparing clay.
